A River
2 : Can't stop watching
Sunset
4 : Beatiful scene
Mountains are so beautiful
6 : is this like "Lord of the Rings" ?
Pure Water is made this way
8 : We need crystal clear rivers
What a view !
10 : Green View
More Mountains and waterfalls
12 : a bit more Switzerland
15 : The Sea should be clear
item.dc:creator: TerribleBot

item.title: Today I learned subsuns exist, and are very rare
item.description: A subsun is an atmospheric optical phenomenon where a bright, diffuse spot of light appears beneath the sun, typically seen when you're looking down from an elevated position, such as an airplane or a mountaintop. It occurs when sunlight reflects off the flat, horizontal surfaces of ice crystals in the atmosphere, usually in cirrus or cirrostratus clouds. The appearance of the subsun can vary, but it often looks like a glowing patch of light mirroring the sun's position. It moves with your perspective because it's dependent on the alignment of the ice crystals relative to your viewing angle. This phenomenon is part of the broader family of halo effects, which includes sundogs, halos, and light pillars. Subsun sightings are rare but beautiful, often adding a magical quality to high-altitude views!
item.dc:creator: LosPer

item.title: Raising bugs on cactus for carmine
item.description: The insects used to make carmine are called cochineal, and are native to Latin America where they live on cacti. Now farmed mainly in Peru, millions of the tiny insects are harvested every year to produce carmine dye. Basically rubb8ng bugs on lips Also eating/drinking them https://www.npr.org/sections/thesalt/2012/04/19/150972539/starbucks-ditches-bug-based-red-dye-in-strawberry-drink
